title: Internship Skill Analysis
text:
Repo: https://github.com/Lucas-Gates/internship_skill_analysis

Summary
This project analyzes early-career job postings to identify in-demand skills and statistically meaningful technical stacks.


Key Results

Python and SQL are the most common technical skills
Distinct stacks appear:

BI: SQL Server + Power BI
ML: PyTorch + TensorFlow
Data Engineering: Hadoop + Spark




Example Visualizations

title: Soundtrack Safeguard
text:
Repo: https://github.com/Lucas-Gates/SoundtrackSafeguard

Problem
In classrooms or school events, playlists can accidentally include songs with inappropriate language or content. Teachers often want a fast way to screen and control what gets played.


What I built
A desktop application that helps make Spotify safer for classroom use by scanning playlists and using user-defined restrictions.

The app launches via main.py using Tkinter
Playlist_Scanner.py fetches playlist + track metadata via the Spotify API
OpenAI_app.py evaluates song content against restrictions (and supports saved settings)
Spotify_Song_Adder.py uses Spotipy OAuth to add approved songs to playlists



Key features

Playlist scanning and filtering workflow
Configurable restrictions (artists/words/settings)
Saved preferences for faster repeat use
Spotify integration (read playlist + modify playlist)



Skills demonstrated

Python desktop UI (Tkinter)
API integration (Spotify)
OAuth authentication flow (Spotipy/Flask)
Responsible filtering logic + saved settings

title: Internship Skill Analysis
text:
Repo: https://github.com/Lucas-Gates/internship_skill_analysis

Problem
Early-career data roles have inconsistent job titles and messy skill labels. It’s hard to quickly understand what skills and “stacks” are most common for internships/entry-level roles.


The Project
An end-to-end Python + R analysis pipeline that: - merges job postings, extracted skills, and summaries using job_link - filters for early-career roles (intern/new grad/entry-level keywords) - normalizes skill labels using alias mapping + whitespace/case cleaning - categorizes skills (Language/Database/Cloud/ML/DE/Viz/Stats/Soft Skills) - computes skill co-occurrence and lift (association strength) - generates polished R visuals for reporting


Example outputs

Category prevalence across postings (what % mention Databases, Visualization, etc.)
Top skills (SQL, Python, etc.)
Lift-based stacks



Skills demonstrated

Data pipeline engineering (joins, cleaning, reproducible outputs)
Association analysis (lift) and support filtering
R visualization (ggplot2) and communication



Example Visualizations

title: Fast Food Data Comparison
text:
Repo: https://github.com/Lucas-Gates/fast-food-analysis

Problem
People compare fast food chains using opinions, but there’s a lot of measurable data: - nutrition per item - price/value - reviews


The Project
A data pipeline that collects and compares fast-food chains using multiple sources, then stores everything in a SQLite database for analysis.

collect_data.py includes web scraping logic (menus/prices/calories) and external data collection
store_data.py builds a normalized SQLite database (final.db) and loads datasets into tables
analyze_data.py queries the DB and produces comparison plots (nutrition metrics, etc.)



Key Features

Multi-source ingestion (web + CSV + DB)
Analysis driven by SQL queries
Visual comparison outputs



Skills demonstrated

Web scraping (BeautifulSoup + requests)
Database design + ETL into SQLite
Analytics using SQL + Python
Visualization and interpretation



Example Visualizations

title: Missouri Budget Graph Maker
text:
Repo: https://github.com/Lucas-Gates/mo-gov-budget-graph-ui

Problem
Budget/expenditure data is often published as large spreadsheets that are difficult to explore quickly without writing code or manually filtering.


The Project
A lightweight desktop GUI (Tkinter) that loads Missouri state expenditure data and lets a user generate charts with a few clicks.

control_panel.py implements a Tkinter control panel UI
graph.py loads a CSV (for example: 2024_State_Expenditures.csv) and generates Matplotlib bar charts
main.py launches the app



Key features

GUI-driven filtering/sorting
Fast category exploration
Automatically generates readable bar charts



Skills demonstrated

Python desktop UI (Tkinter)
Data wrangling in pandas
Visualization in Matplotlib



Example Visualizations

title: Missouri Budget Graph Maker
text:

A lightweight desktop GUI (Tkinter) that loads Missouri state expenditure data and lets a user generate charts with a few clicks.

control_panel.py implements a Tkinter control panel UI
graph.py loads a CSV (for example: 2024_State_Expenditures.csv) and generates Matplotlib bar charts
main.py launches the app





GUI-driven filtering/sorting

Automatically generates readable bar charts

Project structure + reproducible run entrypoint
